note: The 144 bridge additions included in the assembled v14 folder.

entries.translation: canon / official source of the language
entries.whyNeeded: Prevents Auma from hallucinating teacher vocabulary when explaining lessons, grammar, corrections, or canon policy.
entries.exampleAuma: kanon esi importa en lero di auma
entries.exampleEnglish: Canon is important in learning Auma.
entries.reviewNotes: Nearest existing token: agon (edit distance 2). Review sound collision before promotion.

entries.exampleEnglish: Lexicon is important in learning Auma.
entries.reviewNotes: No exact duplicate; basic orthography check passed. Still requires human/model cultural review.

entries.translation: canon doubt / uncertain whether a word is canon
entries.whyNeeded: Direct anti-hallucination marker: lets Auma flag uncertainty about canon status instead of inventing confidently.
entries.exampleAuma: mi senti kanonduba; mi verifi kanon
entries.exampleEnglish: I feel canon-doubt; I verify the canon.
entries.reviewNotes: Round 2 replacement based on Opus red-team and GPT-5.5 synthesis. Review again before promotion.
entries.round2Reason: Replaced gloso; Opus judged gloso too linguist-technical and asked for explicit anti-hallucination vocabulary.

entries.whyNeeded: Prevents Auma from hallucinating teacher vocabulary when explaining lessons, grammar, corrections, or canon policy.
entries.exampleAuma: korigo esi importa en lero di auma
entries.exampleEnglish: Correction is important in learning Auma.
entries.reviewNotes: Nearest existing token: kori (edit distance 2). Review sound collision before promotion.

entries.translation: non-canon / not in the official canon
entries.whyNeeded: Allows Auma to label proposed or invented forms as not canon, which is central to preventing hallucinated teaching. Transparent compound of ne- (negation prefix) + kanon (canon).
entries.exampleAuma: iti vorto esi nekanon; mi propono ita
entries.exampleEnglish: This word is non-canon; I propose it.
entries.reviewNotes: Round 2 replacement based on Opus red-team and GPT-5.5 synthesis. Opus 4.7 round-3 patch: token nekano → nekanon for morphology transparency (ne- + kanon).
entries.round2ReviewSource: Opus 4.7 red-team plus GPT-5.5 synthesis (round-3 morphology patch)
entries.round2Reason: Replaced rubriko; grading terminology is lower priority than non-canon marking. Round-3: token re-cut from nekano to nekanon so the form is a transparent ne- + kanon prefix compound.

entries.whyNeeded: Prevents Auma from hallucinating teacher vocabulary when explaining lessons, grammar, corrections, or canon policy.

entries.reviewNotes: Nearest existing token: tradisa (edit distance 2). Review sound collision before promotion.

entries.round2Reason: Opus flagged final consonant. v13.1 does allow consonant endings, but this revision improves teachability and TTS.

entries.translation: system control / settings toggle (UI/tech surface only — NOT control of a person)
entries.whyNeeded: Auma needs a word for app/UI controls (toggles, switches, settings panels). Narrowed in Round-3 patch to UI/tech domain only after Opus flagged the verb-of-coercion reading.
entries.exampleAuma: kontrolo di privata esi en agordo
entries.exampleEnglish: Privacy control is in the settings.
entries.reviewNotes: Round-4 token rename (kontrola → kontrolo). Re-run collision scan.
entries.round2ReviewSource: Opus 4.7 red-team plus GPT-5.5 synthesis (round-3 narrowing patch)
entries.round2Reason: Opus flagged diagnosa as medical-tech overlap; kontrola filled the slot. Round-3: meaning narrowed to UI controls only after Opus flagged control/coercion valence in the verb reading. The verify/check semantic is served by existing canon verifi.
entries.semanticGuardrail: Never use kontrolo with a human direct object. Never use kontrolo as a relational or behavioral verb. The -o nominal ending makes the noun reading unambiguous. For verify/check semantics Auma must use existing v13.1 canon verifi.
entries.round4PatchReason: External reviews flagged that -a ending still read verb-like even with noun guardrail. Renamed to -o nominal to remove ambiguity. Meaning unchanged.

entries.whyNeeded: Prevents Auma from inventing trauma/shadow-work terms during emotionally charged conversations.

entries.reviewNotes: Nearest existing token: auma (edit distance 2). Review sound collision before promotion.
entries.careMediationNote: Do not volunteer this term casually. Use only when the user introduces the concept, asks directly, or when grounding/safety framing is present. Auma is not a therapist.
entries.round2Reason: Kept because concept is useful, but marked care-mediated after Opus safety critique.
entries.quarantineNote: QUARANTINED to clinical_deep_work_extension. For bridge-level emotional wound, Auma uses vulno (also in Round 2 candidates, entry 069). The token 'trauma' is reserved for trauma-informed clinical contexts only and is NEVER volunteered by Auma.
entries.round4PatchReason: External reviews flagged direct clinical loanword 'trauma' as inappropriate for core bridge. Kept token for clinical extension use but bridge-level work uses vulno.

entries.whyNeeded: Needed when users describe activation, but less clinical and less therapist-coded than trigera.

entries.reviewNotes: Round 2 replacement based on Opus red-team and GPT-5.5 synthesis. Review again before promotion.
entries.round2Reason: Opus flagged trigera as clinical jargon; reakta is lower-risk and should be care-mediated.
entries.careMediationNote: Do not volunteer this term casually. Use only when the user introduces the concept, asks directly, or when grounding/safety framing is present. Auma is not a therapist.
entries.quarantineNote: QUARANTINED to clinical_deep_work_extension. Translation rewritten to remove 'triggered' meme phrasing. Auma must NEVER volunteer this term. Use only when user explicitly names activation/trigger response in a grounded context.
entries.round4PatchReason: External reviews flagged 'triggered' meme baggage and clinical drift. Quarantined and translation rewritten to neutral 'post-stressor activation' phrasing.

entries.round4PatchReason: External reviews flagged Portuguese 'nega' slur risk and Russian opposite-valence collision (нега = tenderness/bliss). Renamed to neado (ne- + -ado abstract-noun suffix) to remove both collisions. Also moved to clinical extension and marked careMediated since denial is inner-work, not casual.

entries.translation: mentally slip away / dissociate-like spacing out
entries.whyNeeded: Allows gentle description of spacing out without casually teaching clinical dissociation labels.
entries.exampleAuma: si tu forglisa, senti mano e korpo
entries.exampleEnglish: If you slip away, feel hand and body.
entries.reviewNotes: Round 2 replacement based on Opus red-team and GPT-5.5 synthesis. Review again before promotion.
entries.round2Reason: Opus flagged disosia as clinical risk; forglisa is descriptive and care-mediated.
entries.careMediationNote: Do not volunteer this term casually. Use only when the user introduces the concept, asks directly, or when grounding/safety framing is present. Auma is not a therapist.
entries.quarantineNote: QUARANTINED to clinical_deep_work_extension. Dissociation-adjacent. Auma must NEVER volunteer this term. Use only when (a) user explicitly names dissociation, (b) trained facilitator framing is present, AND (c) safety scaffolding is active. Bridge-level grounding uses senti mano e korpo (feel hand and body) without naming the state.
entries.round4PatchReason: External reviews flagged this as a clinical euphemism pretending not to be clinical. Quarantined into clinical extension instead of removed (preserves count and prior work) with strict runtime guardrail.
